title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1 – 6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1 – 6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends, a man and a woman, talking about a recent visit to a modern art gallery.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Well, that was... certainly an experience. I’m still trying to process that room with the flickering lightbulbs. Man: Oh, come on, Hollie! [1]It was a profound commentary on the transience of human existence[/1]. Didn't you feel the tension? Woman: Honestly? I just felt like I was in a hardware store with a faulty circuit. I think some of these artists are just seeing how much they can get away with. Man: [2]That’s a bit cynical, isn't it?[/2] Art is meant to provoke a reaction, and it clearly succeeded with you. Woman: A headache isn't the kind of reaction I usually look for in a gallery. I prefer something that shows a bit of technical mastery, you know? Something that didn't look like it was assembled in five minutes. Man: But the concept is the mastery! The execution is secondary to the idea behind it. Woman: I suppose. But I think I’ll stick to the Impressionists next time.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Well, that was... certainly an experience. I’m still trying to process that room with the flickering lightbulbs.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Oh, come on, Hollie! It was a profound commentary on the transience of human existence. Didn't you feel the tension?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Honestly? I just felt like I was in a hardware store with a faulty circuit. I think some of these artists are just seeing how much they can get away with.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That’s a bit cynical, isn't it? Art is meant to provoke a reaction, and it clearly succeeded with you.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: A headache isn't the kind of reaction I usually look for in a gallery. I prefer something that shows a bit of technical mastery, you know? Something that didn't look like it was assembled in five minutes.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: But the concept is the mastery! The execution is secondary to the idea behind it.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suppose. But I think I’ll stick to the Impressionists next time.

questions.question: The man interprets the lightbulb exhibit as...

options.B: a reflection on the temporary nature of life.

questions.explanation: He uses the phrase 'transience of human existence', which is a direct synonym for the temporary nature of life.

questions.question: How does the man react to the woman's opinion?

options.A: He agrees that the exhibit was poorly constructed.
options.B: He suggests she is being overly skeptical.
options.C: He admits he also found the exhibit confusing.

questions.explanation: He asks 'That's a bit cynical, isn't it?', indicating he finds her view too dismissive or skeptical.

extracts.context: You hear two professionals discussing a recent corporate merger.
extracts.audio_script: Man: The news of the merger has certainly sent shockwaves through the department. Woman: It was inevitable, really. We couldn't keep competing with the tech giants on our own. Man: I suppose, but [3]I’m concerned about the clash of corporate cultures[/3]. They’re very 'top-down', whereas we’ve always been more collaborative. Woman: [4]That’s a valid point, and I suspect there will be some friction in the early stages[/4] of the integration. But look at the resources they bring to the table. Man: Oh, the R&D budget is impressive, no doubt. I just hope our core values don't get lost in the shuffle. Woman: We'll have to be proactive. If we can demonstrate that our approach leads to better results, they might even adopt some of our practices. Man: I admire your optimism, Sonia. I just see a lot of 'restructuring' on the horizon, which is usually code for something else. Woman: Let's not jump to conclusions yet.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: The news of the merger has certainly sent shockwaves through the department.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It was inevitable, really. We couldn't keep competing with the tech giants on our own.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I suppose, but I’m concerned about the clash of corporate cultures. They’re very 'top-down', whereas we’ve always been more collaborative.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That’s a valid point, and I suspect there will be some friction in the early stages of the integration. But look at the resources they bring to the table.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Oh, the R&D budget is impressive, no doubt. I just hope our core values don't get lost in the shuffle.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: We'll have to be proactive. If we can demonstrate that our approach leads to better results, they might even adopt some of our practices.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I admire your optimism, Sonia. I just see a lot of 'restructuring' on the horizon, which is usually code for something else.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Let's not jump to conclusions yet.

questions.question: What is the man's primary worry regarding the merger?

options.A: A reduction in the research budget.
options.B: Incompatibility between the two companies' management styles.
options.C: The possibility of a hostile takeover by a tech giant.

questions.explanation: He mentions the 'clash of corporate cultures', comparing their 'top-down' approach with his company's 'collaborative' style.

questions.question: What does the woman concede about the upcoming integration?

options.A: It will likely involve initial conflict.
options.B: It is unlikely to be successful in the long term.
options.C: It will result in immediate job losses.

questions.explanation: She agrees there will be 'some friction in the early stages', which is an advanced way of saying conflict or disagreement.

extracts.context: You hear two students talking about their elective history course.
extracts.audio_script: Man: How did you find the lecture on the Industrial Revolution? Woman: To be honest, [5]I found the lecturer's focus on economic statistics a bit narrow[/5]. I was hoping for more on the social impact. Man: I actually thought the data provided a really solid foundation. It’s hard to understand the social changes without seeing the numbers first. Woman: Maybe, but it was so dry! I struggled to keep my eyes open by the end. Man: Really? [6]I was fascinated by the way he linked the growth of the textile industry to the expansion of the canal network[/6]. It made perfect sense. Woman: Well, I’m glad one of us enjoyed it. I just felt it lacked a human element. It was all about output and GDP, rather than the people living through it. Man: I think next week's seminar is specifically on urban living conditions, so you might find that more up your street. Woman: I hope so, otherwise this is going to be a long term.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: To be honest, I found the lecturer's focus on economic statistics a bit narrow. I was hoping for more on the social impact.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I actually thought the data provided a really solid foundation. It’s hard to understand the social changes without seeing the numbers first.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe, but it was so dry! I struggled to keep my eyes open by the end.

dialogue.speaker: woman

dialogue.text: I hope so, otherwise this is going to be a long term.

questions.question: What was the woman's problem with the lecture?

options.A: She found the lecturer's voice irritating.
options.B: She felt the scope of the material was too limited.
options.C: She thought the information was factually incorrect.

questions.explanation: She describes the focus on statistics as 'a bit narrow', indicating it didn't cover enough breadth (specifically social impact).

questions.question: The man was particularly impressed by the lecturer's ability to...

options.B: make predictions about future industrial trends.
options.C: demonstrate the relationship between different sectors.

questions.explanation: He mentions the link between the 'textile industry' and the 'canal network', showing how different sectors connected.
